# Spike captures — raw `claude -p` output

This directory holds **raw `claude -p --output-format=stream-json` captures** taken for time-boxed spikes. It is intentionally separate from the proper version directories (`v2.1.104/`, `v2.1.148/`, etc.) because:

- The proper catalog reflects the **post-tugcast / post-tugcode** layer that `CodeSessionStore` consumes, with placeholder normalization (`{{uuid}}`, `{{iso}}`, `{{text:len=N}}`) so re-captures are byte-identical.
- Spike captures are the **raw upstream** `claude -p` stream — unnormalized, single-run, no schema. They exist to answer a specific design question in a specific commit, not to gate drift regression.

If a spike capture proves load-bearing for an ongoing test, promote it to a proper probe-table entry per the runbook in `../README.md` and re-capture through the framework. Otherwise leave it here as a frozen artifact.

## Files

| File | Captured | Spike | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|-------|
| `test-task-tools-c-calculator-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-23 | Step 24.1 — TodoList → pinned `Z2A` rework | Documents the Anthropic-side rename from `TodoWrite` (single atomic-replacement tool, ≤ v2.1.112) to the `TaskCreate` / `TaskUpdate` family (per-item CRUD, ≥ v2.1.148). See `roadmap/dev-assistant-rendering.md` § Step 24.1 for the findings derived from this capture. |
| `test-monitor-wake-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 1 — wake event-shape characterization | 90-line capture of `claude -p --output-format=stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --permission-mode=bypassPermissions` running a 5s-timeout Monitor that times out and resumes. Documents the wake signal in stream-json format: a `{type:"system", subtype:"task_notification", task_id, tool_use_id, status, summary, output_file}` event (matches `SDKTaskNotificationMessage` at `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/sdk.d.ts:1659-1668`) appears between the prior turn's `result` and the wake turn's first `message_start`. The wake turn's final `result` carries `origin:{kind:"task-notification"}` as a retrospective marker. See `roadmap/tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` § Step 1 for the full [Q01] resolution derived from this capture. |
| `test-bash-runbg-wake-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort A** | 96-line capture. Bash with `run_in_background:true` + idle wait (no BashOutput poll). Fires `system/task_notification(status:"completed")` and resumes with a wake turn — IDENTICAL wire signature to Monitor's task-notification cohort. **CORRECTS the original Step-1 "negative result" claim**: Bash run_in_background DOES fire task_notification when claude idles (not actively polls). The earlier negative was an artifact of the prompt instructing claude to call BashOutput. See [Q01] for the corrected three-cohort taxonomy. |
| `test-task-runbg-idle-wake-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort A** | 66-line capture. Task with `run_in_background:true` + idle wait (no TaskOutput poll). Fires TWO consecutive `system/task_notification` events (child Bash + parent Agent), one `system/task_progress` intermediate update, and resumes with a wake turn. Cohort A behavior. Also documents the new `task_progress` subtype that tugdeck currently drops; future Slice 2 chrome may surface it. |
| `test-task-runbg-poll-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort C** | 74-line capture. Task with `run_in_background:true` + active TaskOutput polling. Emits `task_started`/`task_updated` but NO `task_notification`. No wake. Belongs to Cohort C (active-polling, not a wake source). Confirms the distinction is "does claude idle?" not "is the tool background?". |
| `test-taskoutput-blocking-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort C** | 67-line capture. TaskOutput blocking wait against a backgrounded subagent. The inner Bash spawned by the subagent fires `task_notification` (consumed by the subagent's own SDK), but the outer claude blocks inside the TaskOutput tool — no outer wake. Cohort C (blocking, not a wake source). |
| `test-schedulewakeup-streamio-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort B** | 30-line capture taken by `probes/probe-sw-streamio.mjs` — spawns claude with `--input-format=stream-json --output-format=stream-json` (same flags tugcode uses), holds subprocess open 90 s after a 60 s ScheduleWakeup. **ZERO output after the initial `result/success`** — the harness re-invoke does NOT fire in tugcode's spawn mode. Tool result text reads *"Next wakeup scheduled… the harness re-invokes you when the wakeup fires or a task-notification arrives."* Confirms Cohort B requires tugcode-owned scheduling (see [D05] / Slice 1b). |
| `test-croncreate-streamio-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort B** | 58-line capture. One-shot CronCreate scheduled for ~1 min in the future via `claude -p`. The `claude -p` invocation exits cleanly after the scheduling tool returns — same Cohort B finding as ScheduleWakeup. Tool result text reads *"Scheduled one-shot task X… Session-only (not written to disk, **dies when Claude exits**)."* Confirms cron jobs are session-scoped per claude's own design. |
| `test-pushnotif-schema-raw.jsonl` | `claude 2.1.150`, 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — cohort sweep, **Cohort C** | 35-line capture probing PushNotification's schema. Synchronous outbound: schema is `{message: string, status: "proactive"}`. Returns a single notification result; no async behavior. Cohort C (not a wake source). |
| `probes/probe-sw-streamio.mjs` | 2026-05-24 | `tugplan-dev-session-wake.md` Step 6 — Cohort B probe harness | Node.js script that spawns `claude` exactly as tugcode does (`--input-format=stream-json --output-format=stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --replay-user-messages --permission-mode=bypassPermissions`), writes a single user_message frame on stdin, and holds the subprocess open 90 s past a 60 s ScheduleWakeup. Used to produce `test-schedulewakeup-streamio-raw.jsonl`. Re-run with `node probes/probe-sw-streamio.mjs` to re-verify the Cohort B finding on a future claude release. |
